_meta.description: Tracking artifact for Geometric Algebra exploration of Observer-Centrism derivations

candidates.title: Spin-Statistics via Cl(3,0) Rotors
candidates.rationale: SU(2)/SO(3) double cover is transparent via Cl(3,0) rotors. The existing derivation uses pi_1(SO(3)) = Z_2 and SU(2) representation theory; GA makes rotors and the double cover algebraically explicit.
candidates.gaInsight: Rotors R in Cl+(3,0) are isomorphic to SU(2). Since R and -R produce the same rotation, the double cover is visible. The spin-statistics connection (integer vs half-integer spin) maps to even/odd grade structure.

candidates.title: Lorentz Group via STA Rotors
candidates.rationale: Spacetime Algebra = Cl(1,3) was designed for this. Rotors unify boosts and rotations into a single algebraic object — both are elements of the even subalgebra differing only in the grade-2 element used.
candidates.gaInsight: Every Lorentz transformation is a rotor R = exp(-B theta/2) where B is a bivector. Spacelike B gives rotations, timelike B gives boosts. Time dilation and length contraction emerge from rotor action on basis vectors.

candidates.title: CPT as a Single Cl(1,3) Object
candidates.rationale: P, T, C map to specific Cl(1,3) operations. CPT becomes a single algebraic element (the pseudoscalar I = e0123) whose commutativity with even-grade elements is immediate.
candidates.gaInsight: In STA: P = spatial inversion (e0 reflection), T = time reversal (e123), C = spinor conjugation. CPT = I = e0123 (unit pseudoscalar). CPT invariance becomes: the pseudoscalar commutes with all even-grade physics.

candidates.title: Maxwell Equations as nabla F = J
candidates.rationale: The electromagnetic field F = E + iB is a bivector in STA. Maxwell's four equations compress to a single equation nabla F = J. Charge quantization maps to quantized bivector rotations.
candidates.gaInsight: The electromagnetic field tensor F_{mu nu} is naturally a grade-2 element (bivector). The vector derivative nabla acting on F splits into divergence and curl parts, recovering all four Maxwell equations from one expression.

candidates.title: Weak Interaction via H = Cl+(3,0)
candidates.rationale: The quaternion algebra H is isomorphic to Cl+(3,0). The three imaginary units I, J, K correspond to bivectors e23, e31, e12. SU(2) gauge transformations are rotor transformations in the even subalgebra.
candidates.gaInsight: Non-commutativity of the weak interaction reduces to the non-commutativity of bivector products in Cl(3,0). The quaternionic closure of the derivation's Theorem 2.1 becomes: the geometric product of different bivectors does not commute.

candidates.title: Chirality as Grade Structure
candidates.rationale: GA makes chirality explicit through grade structure. Left/right projectors (1 +/- gamma_5)/2 become (1 +/- I)/2 where I is the pseudoscalar. Chirality selection is grade-projection selection.
candidates.gaInsight: The pseudoscalar I = e0123 defines chirality: it splits spinors into left-handed (eigenvalue +1) and right-handed (eigenvalue -1) components. The weak interaction coupling only to left-handed fermions becomes: it couples only to one eigenspace of I.

candidates.title: Cayley-Dickson vs Clifford Relationship
candidates.rationale: GA explains why the associativity ceiling terminates at O. The isomorphism C tensor O = Cl(6) is already referenced in anomaly-cancellation and standard-model-group. The Cayley-Dickson construction parallels Clifford doubling but diverges at step 4.
candidates.gaInsight: Clifford algebras are always associative. Cayley-Dickson algebras lose associativity at the octonions. This divergence illuminates bootstrap-division-algebras Gap 4 and explains why the gauge hierarchy terminates.
existingGAReferences: gauge/standard-model-group (line 136): Cl(6) has natural U(1) x SU(3) action
interactions/bootstrap-division-algebras (line 215): Open gap on Clifford algebras

candidates.title: Entanglement via Rotor Pairs
candidates.rationale: Entangled states correspond to non-factorizable elements in the product algebra. No-cloning follows from GA product rules. Entanglement monogamy maps to bivector sharing constraints.
candidates.gaInsight: Two-particle entanglement is represented by a non-separable element in the product algebra. The no-cloning theorem has a clean GA interpretation: cloning would require duplicating a rotor, violating the geometric product structure.

candidates.title: ER=EPR via STA Geometry
candidates.rationale: STA provides natural geometric language for wormhole throat geometry. The duality between EPR (bivector entanglement) and ER (rotor-generated curvature) becomes transparent in Cl(1,3).
candidates.gaInsight: The throat geometry of an Einstein-Rosen bridge has a natural STA description as a minimal bivector surface. The ER=EPR duality maps to a structural equivalence between entanglement (algebraic) and topology (geometric) in the same algebra.

candidates.title: Gravity in STA: Gauge Theory Gravity
candidates.rationale: Gauge theory gravity (Lasenby, Doran, Gull) reformulates GR in flat-space STA with position and rotation gauge fields. Geodesics become rotor equations.
candidates.gaInsight: In gauge theory gravity, the gravitational field is described by two gauge fields in flat STA background. The equivalence principle becomes a gauge symmetry statement. Gravitational redshift (Theorem 2.1) has a clean rotor derivation.

candidates.title: Einstein Equations in STA Form
candidates.rationale: The Riemann tensor as a bivector-to-bivector mapping; Einstein equations as G(a) = kT(a) where G and T are linear functions on vectors. Lovelock uniqueness restated in GA language.
candidates.gaInsight: The Riemann tensor R(B) maps bivectors to bivectors. The Einstein equation operates on vectors rather than index-laden tensors. Lovelock's uniqueness theorem (existing Theorem 3.2) gains a clean GA proof.

filteredOut.reason: More about probability theory (Gleason's theorem) than geometry
Core content is relational, not geometrical
Mostly coherence accounting with spacetime as background
